strengths: Ambitious and physically-motivated problem formulation addressing the non-Lambertian bottleneck in light field depth estimation, with MRI-inspired k-space frequency analysis as a creative conceptual framing
Lightweight architecture (~0.21M params) processing the full 9×9 angular view, which is practically relevant for real deployment scenarios
Honest discussion of computational limitations and angular aliasing constraints in the Conclusion, showing scientific maturity regarding the method's boundary conditions
weaknesses: Critical disconnect between claimed innovations and actual methodology: the abstract/intro promise dual-mask BRDF modeling, k-space analysis, and SAM-guided optimization, but the Methodology section only formalizes a standard L1 photometric cost volume with a U-Net decoder—none of the headline contributions are mathematically instantiated

Experimental section contains only setup details (datasets, hyperparameters, loss formulation) with no visible quantitative results tables, SOTA comparison numbers, ablation studies, or visual depth map figures—the claimed MAE=0.089 and 38.5% improvement appear only in the Conclusion with no supporting evidence in the Experiments section

Baseline MAE values are inconsistent across sections: Introduction cites 0.081 (Lambertian) and 0.411 (non-Lambertian), while Conclusion cites a plateau of 0.28–0.34—these cannot refer to the same experimental conditions without clarification
key_gaps_vs_tcsvt: TCSVT requires complete experimental validation with quantitative comparison tables against ≥5 SOTA methods across multiple benchmarks with BadPix, MSE, and MAE metrics—this paper's Experiments section contains only implementation details and no results

The core methodological formalization is missing: no equations for the k-space angular frequency transform, constrained least-squares BRDF estimation, dual-mask construction, GCD computation, or SAM-guided energy optimization—all of which are claimed as primary contributions

Citation count (~25-28 unique references) is below the expected threshold for a TCSVT paper; Related Work section uses generic <citation> placeholders rather than proper \cite{} commands for several key prior works (Jeon, Shin, Zhu)
